name: DevOps Pipeline
description: Infrastructure and automation focused AGENTS.md for teams managing cloud infrastructure.
content:
# AGENTS.md

> This file defines the rules and conventions for AI agents working in this infrastructure-as-code repository.

---

## Table of Contents

- [Terraform Module Structure](#terraform-module-structure)
- [Terraform State Management](#terraform-state-management)
- [Terraform Security Rules](#terraform-security-rules)
- [Ansible Playbook Conventions](#ansible-playbook-conventions)
- [Ansible Variable Management](#ansible-variable-management)
- [Ansible Role Testing](#ansible-role-testing)
- [Secrets Management](#secrets-management)
- [Input Validation & Output Encoding](#input-validation--output-encoding)
- [Documentation Standards](#documentation-standards)

---

## Terraform Module Structure

Use the standard layout: `infra/environments/{env}/` for environment roots and `infra/modules/` for reusable modules. Pin provider versions. Every module has `main.tf`, `variables.tf`, `outputs.tf`, and `README.md`. Tag all resources with `Project`, `Environment`, `ManagedBy = "terraform"`, and `Owner`.

---

## Terraform State Management

All state is remote (S3 + DynamoDB). Never commit `.tfstate` to git. All production applies happen through CI/CD. Use `moved {}` blocks for refactoring. Schedule nightly drift detection.

---

## Terraform Security Rules

No hardcoded secrets. Use `sensitive = true` on secret variables. IAM policies must be least-privilege — no `Action: *` or `Resource: *`. Run `tfsec` and `checkov` in CI. No security group allows `0.0.0.0/0` except on 80/443.

---

## Ansible Playbook Conventions

All tasks must be idempotent. Use roles for reusable logic. Prefer built-in modules over `shell:`. Every task has a `name:`. Pin collection versions in `requirements.yml`. Use `become: true` only at the task level.

---

## Ansible Variable Management

Use Ansible's variable precedence intentionally. All variables are `snake_case` and prefixed with the role name. Never store plaintext secrets in group_vars. Use Ansible Vault or HashiCorp Vault for secrets.

---

## Ansible Role Testing

Every role has a Molecule test scenario. Run `ansible-lint`, `yamllint`, and `molecule test` in CI on every PR that modifies a role.

---

## Secrets Management

Never hardcode secrets. Never commit secrets to git. Use AWS Secrets Manager or HashiCorp Vault. Run `gitleaks` pre-commit and in CI. Enable GitHub secret scanning and push protection. Rotate secrets on schedule.

---

## Input Validation & Output Encoding

Validate all inputs at trust boundaries. Use allowlists, not denylists. Enforce numeric bounds and string length limits. Never construct shell commands from user input.

---

## Documentation Standards

Every module and role must have a `README.md` with: purpose, inputs, outputs, and a usage example. Maintain a `CHANGELOG.md`. Update docs in the same PR as the code change.
